protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: cells were harvested, re-suspended in Laemmli buffer, and boiled; samples were resolved for SDS-PAGE and Western blot

protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: Cell pellet was re-suspended in BPER-II; cells were lysed via French pressure, centrifuged, and BMCs were washed

protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: Purified BMC suspension was applied to a grid, stained, dried at room temperature, and analyzed by TEM

protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: Cells were lysed via French pressure; lysates were centrifuged, treated with EBB detergent, purified, and quantified

protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: For purification from isolated BMCs, EBB detergent was used to solubilize PduP-E; fractions were analyzed by SDS-PAGE and Western blot

protocols.Paper_title: Re-directing bacterial microcompartment systems to enhance recombinant expression of lysis protein E from bacteriophage φX174 in Escherichia coli
protocols.Evidence: Factor Xa-treated PduP-E was centrifuged to remove resin; the mixture was directly used in growth inhibition assays
